extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Grader evidence is generally grounded in the expert_memo preview; scoring is internally consistent (weighted sum matches final_score). Main limitation: the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed, so claims about workbook-level formulas/reproducibility cannot be independently verified. No hidden-reference leakage detected.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

issues.description: The grader correctly notes that openpyxl (or equivalent) was unavailable and the .xlsx could not be programmatically inspected. The artifact preview shows parse_status 'openpyxl_unavailable' with preview_error_message 'No module named \u0027openpyxl\u0027'. This prevents automated verification of cell-level formulas and workbook-embedded calculations.
issues.suggested_fix: Re-run verification in an environment with openpyxl (or provide an exported calc_trace.csv / sheet showing formulas as text) so spreadsheet formulas and row-level citations can be validated.
issues.title: Spreadsheet parsing unavailable

issues.description: The grader claims the memo's numeric calculations 'independently recompute correctly' and that spreadsheet calculations are consistent, but because the workbook could not be parsed, this assertion is based only on the memo preview. The claim that the workbook itself exposes formulas and reproduces results cannot be confirmed.
issues.suggested_fix: Either enable programmatic parsing of the workbook or include an explicit calculation trace (CSV or text sheet) from the workbook to substantiate that formulas exist and match the memo.
issues.title: Workbook-level reproducibility claim unsupported by available previews

issues.description: The model's computed_semantic_score_breakdown (ri_1=1.0, ri_2=1.0, ri_3=1.0, ri_4=0.8) with weights (0.35,0.25,0.2,0.2) yields a weighted_sum of 0.96 which matches the reported final_score 0.96. This is internally consistent.
issues.title: Score arithmetic consistency check

issues.description: The grader and deterministic precheck both state 'hidden_reference_leaked': false and 'hidden_reference_used': false. Artifact previews contain only the declared artifacts; no hidden-reference content is present in the previews.
issues.title: No hidden-reference leakage detected
